name: Baking Tips
recipeInstructions:
1. Always read through the entire recipe before beginning.
2. Start with ingredients at room temperature.
3. When baking bars, squares, and cookies, place them in the center of the oven on the middle rack. If baking more than one pan at a time, place them at different angles on different racks to allow maximum circulation of heat. Alternate their placement on the racks halfway through the baking time.
4. Use the best pans available, which are typically aluminum or metal and light in color. Dark pans will cause the base or bars to bake and brown more quickly.
5. If using glass dishes, reduce the oven temperature by 25 degrees Fahrenheit and slightly decrease the baking time.
6. Use only the size of pan specified in the recipe. For example, an 8-inch (2-liter) square pan versus a 9-inch (2.5-liter) square pan can result in vastly different outcomes, from moist and chewy bars to underbaked and heavy failures.
7. Check baked goods at the earliest time indicated to avoid overbaking, which can dry out bars and squares.
8. If making a sticky or fluffy batter or base, lightly dampen your hand or spatula before patting or spreading the batter into the pan.
9. Let most bars cool completely in the pan before cutting. However, bars with a sticky filling may require running a knife around the edge of the pan after removal from the oven to prevent sticking.
10. Score chocolate-covered bars as soon as the topping is applied to prevent the chocolate from cracking later.
11. Use metal or plastic dry measuring cups for dry ingredients and glass liquid measuring cups for liquids.
12. All-purpose flour does not need to be sifted, but cake and pastry flour should be sifted before measuring.
13. Store nuts in the freezer to maintain their freshness.
